mappings.rationaleAI: The Manhattan Project covers the development of nuclear weapons during WWII and the bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki, directly matching A.2.d on the role of US forces in defeating the Axis powers and A.3.a on the Truman administration.
Hindenburg's appointment of Hitler relates to the context of US foreign policy and opposition to Japanese expansionism and Axis powers under A.2.c, though the connection is indirect. Reject.
The Haymarket Affair (1886) predates this section's timeframe of since 1920. Reject.
Hayek's Road to Serfdom influenced Reagan's economic policies and neoliberal thought, directly matching A.4.f.i on Reagan's cuts in social welfare programs and conservative economic policies.

The gold standard's history including the Great Depression's deflationary pressures and interwar economics directly relates to A.1.c on Hoover's administration and the stock market crash, and the broader economic context of A.1.a on Coolidge prosperity.

The Sino-Japanese War merged into WWII, covering US opposition to Japanese expansionism and the Pacific war, directly matching A.2.c on opposition to Japanese expansionism and A.2.d on the role of US forces.

mappings.episodeTitle: The Economic Consequences of the Peace
mappings.rationaleAI: Keynes's critique of the Treaty of Versailles reparations provides essential context for the interwar economic order and the conditions leading to the Great Depression, relevant to A.1 on post-WWI developments.
The American Century covers America's rise from isolationism to global superpower, the Cold War, and debates about American exceptionalism, directly matching the entire arc of post-1920 US history.
